hypothesis: fringe_accuracy is low because several skills reference .planning/ paths in their protocols but have no guard for the directory not existing, and orchestrators (evolve, archon) have no timeout fringe case for hung sub-agents
prediction: Adding .planning/ existence guards and hung-agent timeout cases will raise fringe_accuracy >= 0.5 across 3+ skills
falsification: If score does not rise > 0.5 after adding these guards, hypothesis rejected
evidence: session-handoff gained .planning/ guard; evolve gained scout-timeout fringe (10min abort); archon gained hung-agent fringe (30min abort); lint PASS after changes

hypothesis: orientation_precision is low because 6+ skills have no Orientation section (or have one missing 'Don't use when' with neighbor names), causing evaluators to score the skill low on agent routing clarity
prediction: Adding Orientation + Don't-use-when with named neighbors will raise orientation_precision >= 0.5 across 5+ skills
falsification: If score does not rise > 0.5 after adding orientations, hypothesis rejected
evidence: fleet, session-handoff, scaffold, postmortem, learn, organize all gained Orientation sections with named neighbors; lint PASS after changes

hypothesis: output_completeness is low because HANDOFF blocks in several skills use bare prose slots with no typed constraints and are missing the reversibility field that tells the recipient how to undo the skill's effects
prediction: Adding reversibility literals and typed HANDOFF slot keys will raise output_completeness >= 0.5 across 3+ skills
falsification: If score does not rise > 0.5 after typed slots and reversibility, hypothesis rejected
evidence: marshal HANDOFF converted to typed key-value; research gained reversibility literal; postmortem duplicate block removed + reversibility field added; lint PASS after changes

hypothesis: orientation_precision scores 6.5/10 because 13 skills have no Orientation section — evaluators enumerate and find routing ambiguity for archon, daemon, setup, wiki, and 9 others
prediction: Adding Orientation+Don't-use-when with named neighbors will raise orientation_precision >= 0.5 across 12+ skills
falsification: If score does not rise > 0.5 after adding orientations, hypothesis rejected
evidence: 12 skills received Orientation sections with named neighbor skills; lint PASS; bench PASS; no word-count violations after schedule trim

hypothesis: fringe_accuracy scores 6.5/10 because cost, dashboard, refactor, review have no fringe section — evaluators enumerate 5 real failure modes and find none handled
prediction: Adding real-failure-mode fringe cases will raise fringe_accuracy >= 0.5 across 4 skills
falsification: If cases are hypothetical rather than from real failure modes, hypothesis not confirmed
evidence: cost (4 cases), dashboard (4 cases), refactor (5 cases), review (3 cases) all received fringe sections with specific user-facing messages; all cases derived from actual failure modes (missing dirs, malformed JSON, no test baseline, binary files)

hypothesis: escalation_guidance scores 6.0/10 because 30+ skills have zero contextual gates — no reversibility classification, no trust levels, no cost threshold
prediction: Adding testable contextual gates to 9 high-impact skills will raise escalation_guidance >= 1.5
falsification: If gates added are vague ('if the task is large') rather than testable thresholds, hypothesis not confirmed
evidence: 9 skills (architect, create-skill, design, doc-gen, experiment, infra-audit, live-preview, map, prd) received Contextual Gates with green/amber/red reversibility, specific undo commands, named trust tiers; 4 more via cross-pollination

hypothesis: output_completeness scores 6.5/10 because 30+ skills are missing reversibility field in their HANDOFF blocks
prediction: Adding Reversibility field to HANDOFF blocks in 9 skills will raise output_completeness >= 0.5
falsification: If downstream agents still cannot parse reversibility from the HANDOFF, hypothesis not confirmed
evidence: 9 skills received Reversibility bullet in HANDOFF/Exit Protocol blocks; 3 more via cross-pollination (verify, research-fleet, qa)
